title: Case-file criteria aggregation defect & fix (ADR 0001)
content: In the Immigration Paperwork (Meridian) repo, the O-1A case-file summary in CriteriaTable.tsx hardcoded '1 partial' while computing the qualifying count via `status !== 'Partial'` — a status-aggregation correctness defect that silently misreports eligibility when criteria data changes and over-counts on malformed/typo statuses. Fixed by centralizing into a pure summarizeCriteria() aggregator (criteria.ts) counting only exact Met/Strong + Partial, ignoring malformed rows, with QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD=3 driving badge tone. Render unchanged for current data (7/1/8). Pattern to watch for repo-wide: hardcoded summary literals next to computed values (also present: '92% approval likelihood' in CaseFileDashboard.tsx). Repo has no test runner — node:test + `npx tsx --test` is the established convention.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:14 UTC
Case-file review: APPROVE of ADR 0001 criteria aggregation fix + recurring hardcoded-summary pattern
content: Reviewed the case-file criteria-aggregation fix (ADR 0001) in the Immigration Paperwork repo. Verdict: APPROVE with notes. The hardcoded '1 partial' defect is properly resolved via the pure summarizeCriteria() aggregator (criteria.ts) — counts only exact Met/Strong as qualifying and Partial as partial, excludes malformed rows, QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD=3 drives badge tone. Independently verified: tsx --test 4/4 pass, tsc --noEmit clean, Badge tone contract valid. NAMED RECURRING PATTERN for this repo's eligibility surface: hardcoded summary literals sitting next to computed values. Confirmed instances: (1) FIXED — CriteriaTable badge counts; (2) OPEN — '92% approval likelihood' literal in CaseFileDashboard.tsx:56; (3) Low — 'of 8' magic denominator in CriteriaTable.tsx:18. On future case-file reviews, scan for static eligibility/likelihood literals and require them to be data-derived or explicitly marked non-binding. Resolved this run: hardcoded partial count — do NOT re-flag.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:17 UTC
immigration-paperwork security baseline: clean except transitive PostCSS XSS via Next.js
content: First full security scan of the immigration-paperwork repo (Next.js 16/React 19/TS static frontend, no API routes/backend). Result: 0 critical, 0 high, 1 medium, 1 low. Only real finding = moderate transitive PostCSS XSS (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93, postcss<8.5.10) pulled by next ^16.2.6 — build-time only, fixed via `npm update next` (npm's suggested next@9.3.3 downgrade is wrong). No hardcoded secrets, no eval/exec/SQL/dangerouslySetInnerHTML/http://, secrets properly gitignored, data.ts is mock PII only. On re-scan: confirm the PostCSS advisory is fixed; the rest was clean — don't fabricate new findings.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:20 UTC
immigration-paperwork versioning: pre-1.0 rule + v0.2.1 decision
content: Repo CHANGELOG declares a SemVer convention: while pre-1.0 (0.x), only BREAKING changes increment MINOR; features and fixes are PATCH. Decisions on record: v0.2.0 = Next.js 14→16 / React 18→19 / Tailwind 3→4 / TS 5→6 platform upgrade (breaking → minor). v0.2.1 = backward-compatible feature + fix (summarizeCriteria() eligibility helper + CriteriaTable badge no longer hardcodes '1 partial'; dynamic warning/success tone) → PATCH. On the v0.2.1 run, package.json was stale at 0.1.0 while CHANGELOG already had an unfinalized [0.2.0]; reconciled by bumping to 0.2.1, appending [0.2.1] (0.2.0 preserved), tagging v0.2.1 and backfilling v0.2.0 on commit 35486fd. Next release: continue PATCH for features/fixes, MINOR only for breaking, until 1.0.

Immigration Paperwork v0.2.1: docs self-documented by the release commit; version lag resolved
content: On 2026-05-27 a release.version.bumped event for repo 'immigration-paperwork' v0.2.1 (patch) arrived — the first event in a while matching the connected repo AND carrying a new commit (68b2983, after last-processed 35486fd). The commit 'Release 0.2.1: criteria eligibility helper + badge fix' SELF-DOCUMENTED: it added a CHANGELOG [0.2.1] entry alongside the code. Verified that entry against the diff — accurate/complete: Added summarizeCriteria() helper (criteria.ts, qualifying/partial/threshold, robust to malformed input), Fixed CriteriaTable hardcoded '1 partial' badge (now derives counts), Changed badge tone to dynamic success/warning. criteria.test.ts is test-only (skipped). README needed no edit (internal refactor/bugfix; tech stack already current). No doc edits required. IMPORTANT: the long-standing package.json version lag is RESOLVED — it now reads 0.2.1 (was stuck at 0.1.0 across the v0.2.0 runs). Advanced bookmark to 68b2983.
